mnemonics: Show Your Clocks Now, Or Not Yet
keyPoints: All systems must agree on a single time reference point.
Using NTP (Network Time Protocol) for centralized clock synchronization.
Time synchronization protocols can be based on client-server architecture, peer-to-peer architecture, or hybrid models.
The master clock in a network is responsible for sending its time to other clocks in the network.
Clocks can be synchronized in real-time or in a periodic manner, with periodic synchronization reducing the frequency of communication between clocks.
Time synchronization can also be achieved using atomic clocks, which provide accurate and stable time references.
